skill.name: invalid-governance-skill
skill.summary: A test fixture designed to verify governance enforcement failures in the skill marketplace.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: This skill is a test fixture containing only a YAML frontmatter header and a short markdown description with no executable code. The two static findings are false positives: the 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flag was triggered by the word 'validation' in the description, and the 'system reconnaissance' flag was triggered by the word 'Governance' in the heading. Neither represents an actual security threat. The manifest.json has an invalid name field and date format, which is expected for this fixture designed to test governance enforcement.

dangerous_patterns.title: False positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged the word 'validation' in the description field as a weak cryptographic algorithm indicator. This is a false positive — 'validation' here refers to governance validation testing, not cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: False positive: System reconnaissance pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged the word 'Governance' in the heading as a system reconnaissance indicator. This is a false positive — the heading refers to the skill's purpose as a governance test fixture.

content.value_statement: Governance checks for skill manifests often fail silently in production. This test fixture provides a known-bad manifest to verify that governance validation correctly catches policy violations.
actual_capabilities: Provides a known-invalid manifest.json to trigger governance validation failures
Contains an intentionally mismatched name field to test name policy enforcement
Uses a non-standard date format to test date validation rules
Serves as a reference for what governance violations look like
Integrates with automated governance test suites in CI pipelines
limitations: Cannot be used as a functional skill due to invalid manifest
Only useful for governance validation testing purposes
Contains no executable code or runtime functionality

use_cases.title: CI Pipeline Governance Testing
use_cases.description: Use this fixture to verify that automated governance checks catch invalid manifest configurations before deployment.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers building skill marketplace CI pipelines

use_cases.title: Governance Policy Development
use_cases.description: Reference this fixture when developing new governance rules to understand what violation patterns look like.
use_cases.target_user: Platform engineers designing skill governance policies

use_cases.title: Developer Onboarding
use_cases.description: Show new contributors examples of governance failures so they understand what to avoid in their own skill submissions.

prompt_templates.title: Run Governance Validation Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the governance validator against the invalid-governance-skill fixture and report all detected policy violations.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Governance Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare governance validation results between valid-skill and invalid-governance-skill fixtures to verify the validator correctly distinguishes them.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Governance Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Run governance validation on this fixture and generate a detailed report of all violations including the specific manifest field that failed.

prompt_templates.title: Integrate into CI Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow step that runs governance validation against the invalid-governance-skill fixture and fails the build if the fixture passes validation.

output_examples.input: Run governance validation on the invalid-governance-skill fixture.
output: Violation: manifest.name does not match SKILL.md name
Violation: manifest.updated_at uses incorrect date format

output_examples.input: What governance issues exist in this fixture?
output: Issue 1: manifest.json contains name 'wrong-name' which does not match skill name 'invalid-governance-skill'
Issue 2: updated_at field uses '2026/03/31' instead of ISO 8601 format
Status: Fixture is working as intended for governance testing

best_practices: Always run governance validation against known-bad fixtures to verify your validator catches expected violations
Keep governance test fixtures in a dedicated tests/fixtures directory with clear naming conventions
Document each fixture's expected violations so reviewers understand the governance intent
anti_patterns: Do not deploy this fixture to production environments as it contains an intentionally invalid manifest
Do not modify the fixture to make it pass validation, as this defeats its testing purpose
Do not use this fixture as a template for real skills since it violates governance policies by design

faq.question: What is this skill used for?
faq.answer: This is a test fixture for governance validation. It contains an intentionally invalid manifest to verify that governance checks correctly identify policy violations.

faq.question: Why does the manifest have wrong values?
faq.answer: The wrong values are deliberate. The name field does not match the SKILL.md name, and the date format is not ISO 8601. Both violations test specific governance rules.

faq.question: Can this be published to the marketplace?
faq.answer: No. This fixture fails governance validation by design and should not be published. It exists only for testing governance enforcement.

faq.question: Does this skill contain any executable code?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains only a SKILL.md file with a YAML header and a manifest.json with test data. There is no code to execute.

faq.question: Are the security warnings in the audit report real?
faq.answer: No. The warnings are false positives triggered by keywords like 'validation' and 'Governance' in the description text. This fixture contains no security threats.

faq.question: How should I integrate this fixture into my CI pipeline?
faq.answer: Add a step that runs the governance validator against this fixture and asserts that it reports the expected violations. If it passes validation, your governance rules may be too lenient.
